refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound operational data.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage), elapsed time, safety incidents, and policy violations. Calculate productivity_achieved = total_volume / total_time. Verify productivity_achieved ≥ productivity_target and zero safety plan violations.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time and accuracy.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness sufficient for safe, productive remote task execution.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and alert operator/site manager to policy violations in real-time.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect and report productivity telemetry (volume, time, cycle count) for KPI verification.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-capacity vehicle with autonomous task planning and remote operator override; measure productivity via onboard sensors and report via telemetry.
DP-B: Deploy vehicle with manual operator control (on-site or tele-operated); enforce safety constraints via Safety Services; measure productivity via task logs and site surveys.

DP-C: Hybrid approach: vehicle with semi-autonomous task execution (dig-load-dump cycles) guided by operator; real-time telemetry and safety monitoring; productivity measured via integrated sensor suite.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid approach—vehicle equipped with semi-autonomous task execution (dig-load-dump cycles) guided by operator commands (on-site or tele-operated); real-time telemetry collection (volume, cycle time, position) streamed via Network Link; Safety Services monitors compliance and alerts operator/site manager; productivity KPI computed from integrated sensor data and reported in Telemetry interface.

potential_sources: Teleoperation and Vehicle control loops must synchronize cycle timing to avoid operator command latency degrading productivity.
Safety Services constraint enforcement (e.g., geofencing, speed limits) may reduce cycle speed; must be tuned to meet productivity_target without violating safety plan.
Telemetry bandwidth and latency must support real-time safety alerts without blocking task execution.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C decouples operator skill/availability (via semi-autonomy) from raw vehicle capability, reducing coupling between Teleoperation and Vehicle domains. Safety Services is orthogonal (constraint layer). Telemetry is a passive observer, not a control loop. Coupling is manageable via interface contracts.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Semi-autonomous task execution (dig-load-dump) is technically feasible within project timeline and cost.
Assumption: Operator (on-site or remote) can reliably command semi-autonomous cycles and respond to safety alerts.
Risk: If teleoperation latency exceeds ~500 ms, operator may struggle to guide cycles; productivity may degrade below target.
Risk: Safety Services constraints (e.g., reduced speed in populated zones) may conflict with productivity_target in certain worksite conditions.
Risk: Sensor accuracy for volume measurement may introduce ±5–10% error in productivity KPI; acceptance criteria must account for measurement uncertainty.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify productivity_target value and worksite safety plan constraints early. Conduct latency and sensor accuracy trade studies before committing to DP-C. If latency or safety constraints prove incompatible with target, pivot to DP-B (manual control with stricter safety monitoring) or DP-A (higher autonomy, lower operator dependency).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module encompassing Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry collection/reporting. Owns the end-to-end productivity KPI measurement and reporting pipeline. Responsible for coordinating task execution, safety compliance, and data aggregation across sub-modules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution reports productivity KPI (m³/h, ton/h, cycle count, elapsed time) and operational status to Site Manager and Project Manager dashboards. Frequency: real-time or periodic (e.g., every 5 min).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager or Tele-operator sends task commands (dig location, load target, dump zone) to NGHE Solution. NGHE Solution translates to Vehicle and Teleoperation sub-module commands.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution (via Safety Services sub-module) broadcasts safety alerts (geofence violation, speed limit exceeded, policy breach) to Operator and Site Manager. Frequency: real-time on event.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for Teleoperation (operator commands, video/sensor feedback) and Telemetry (productivity data, safety alerts). Supports both on-site (local network) and remote (WAN) operation.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI requirement. All sub-functions (Vehicle control, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Telemetry) are logically contained. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Tasking in, Telemetry/SafetyAlert out, Network Link) cross boundary. Ownership is clear: System Engineer or Project Manager.

Vehicle module owns task execution (dig, load, dump) and onboard sensors (volume, position, cycle time). However, productivity KPI computation, safety constraint enforcement, and telemetry reporting are outside Vehicle scope. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for KPI verification and safety compliance across multiple modules.

Teleoperation owns operator interface and command transmission. Productivity depends on Teleoperation (operator skill, latency), but Teleoperation does not own task execution, sensor data collection, or KPI computation. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would obscure Vehicle and Safety Services dependencies.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and alert generation. Productivity is affected by Safety Services (e.g., speed limits reduce cycle time), but Safety Services does not own task execution or KPI measurement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services alone would be a category error (safety is a constraint, not a productivity driver).

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: System Engineer (or Project Manager as system integrator). NGHE Solution is the integration point; ownership must span Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry sub-modules to ensure end-to-end KPI accountability.
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution module contains the productivity KPI requirement because it owns the integration logic, telemetry pipeline, and coordination of sub-modules. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules that contribute to productivity but do not independently satisfy the KPI requirement. Telemetry is the measurement/reporting mechanism, owned by NGHE Solution.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle module is owned by a different team (e.g., hardware supplier), responsibility for cycle time and sensor accuracy may be unclear. Mitigation: Define Vehicle interface contract (cycle time SLA, sensor accuracy spec) and include in acceptance criteria.

Risk: If Teleoperation latency or Safety Services constraints degrade productivity below target, blame may diffuse across modules. Mitigation: Establish clear performance budgets (e.g., max latency 300 ms, max speed reduction 10%) and allocate to sub-modules.

Risk: Telemetry data quality (sensor noise, missing samples) may make KPI verification unreliable. Mitigation: Define telemetry validation rules and fallback measurement methods (e.g., manual volume surveys).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution module ownership includes responsibility for KPI verification and reporting. Define sub-module interfaces (Vehicle cycle time SLA, Teleoperation latency budget, Safety Services constraint list) and include in design specification. Establish telemetry data quality requirements and validation procedures.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid semi-autonomous + operator guidance + real-time telemetry + safety monitoring) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI). DP-C decouples operator skill from vehicle capability, reduces latency sensitivity via semi-autonomy, and provides measurable telemetry for KPI verification. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Telemetry collection and KPI computation are distributed across Vehicle (sensor data), Teleoperation (operator commands), Safety Services (constraint logs), and NGHE Solution (aggregation). Mitigation: Define a centralized Telemetry module or service within NGHE Solution that owns KPI computation logic.

Diffusion Risk 2: Safety Services constraint enforcement may be implemented in Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services sub-modules. Mitigation: Establish a clear safety constraint interface (e.g., SafetyAlert) that Safety Services owns and other modules consume.

Diffusion Risk 3: Network Link is a shared resource for Teleoperation (command/feedback) and Telemetry (data reporting). Mitigation: Define bandwidth and latency budgets for each use case and monitor in integration testing.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution module is the appropriate container for FR-NGHE-001 because it owns the integration logic and KPI reporting pipeline. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and contribute to productivity via well-defined interfaces. No module boundary violations detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trial. Productivity KPI (m³/h or ton/h) can be computed from telemetry data (volume, time) collected by NGHE Solution. Safety compliance can be verified via SafetyAlert logs and site manager reports. Measurement uncertainty (±5–10% sensor error) must be accounted for in acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution module. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry sub-modules respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Define productivity_target value (e.g., 50 m³/h) and worksite safety plan constraints (e.g., max speed 5 m/s in populated zones) in the acceptance criteria. These are critical inputs to DP-C design and sub-module budgeting.

Revision 2: Establish telemetry data quality requirements (e.g., sensor accuracy ±5%, sample rate ≥ 1 Hz) and validation procedures (e.g., cross-check with manual surveys). Include in NGHE Solution design specification.

Revision 3: Define sub-module interface contracts (Vehicle cycle time SLA, Teleoperation latency budget, Safety Services constraint list) and include in design specification. Allocate performance budgets to sub-modules.

Revision 4: Conduct latency and sensor accuracy trade studies before committing to DP-C. If latency or safety constraints prove incompatible with productivity_target, prepare contingency DPs (DP-A or DP-B).

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (task execution, teleoperation, safety compliance, telemetry) and corresponding DPs and IMs. The refined FR and sub-FRs are well-defined and traceable to NGHE Solution module. DP-C is a sound design approach that balances productivity, safety, and operator dependency. However, sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through 001.4) must be decomposed further to define detailed design parameters for Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry sub-modules. Gate review passes; proceed to next decomposition level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with repeatable cycle time ≤ cycle_time_target and positional accuracy ≥ accuracy_target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall provide operator control and situational awareness (video, sensor feedback) with end-to-end latency ≤ latency_budget and command success rate ≥ 95%.

FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, policy rules) and alert operator/site manager to violations with latency ≤ alert_latency_budget.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall collect, validate, and report productivity telemetry (volume, time, cycle count) with accuracy ≥ 90% and reporting latency ≤ telemetry_latency_budget.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Semi-autonomous task execution (dig-load-dump cycles) is technically feasible within project timeline and cost constraints.
Assumption 2: Operator (on-site or remote) can reliably command semi-autonomous cycles and respond to safety alerts within acceptable latency.
Assumption 3: Onboard sensors (volume, position, cycle time) can measure productivity with ±5–10% accuracy.
Assumption 4: Network Link (local or WAN) can support Teleoperation (command/feedback) and Telemetry (data reporting) simultaneously without exceeding latency budgets.
Assumption 5: Worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, policy rules) can be enforced by Safety Services without reducing productivity below target by >10%.
Assumption 6: Site Manager and Tele-operator are trained and available to operate NGHE Solution during representative operations trial.
open_risks: Risk 1: Teleoperation latency may exceed operator tolerance (>500 ms), degrading productivity. Mitigation: Conduct latency trade study and define latency budget for sub-modules.

Risk 2: Safety Services constraints may conflict with productivity_target in certain worksite conditions (e.g., populated zones with speed limits). Mitigation: Define safety/productivity trade-off matrix and validate in representative operations.
Risk 3: Sensor accuracy for volume measurement may introduce >10% error in productivity KPI. Mitigation: Define sensor accuracy requirements and fallback measurement methods (e.g., manual surveys).

Risk 4: Network Link reliability may be insufficient for remote teleoperation (e.g., WAN outages). Mitigation: Define network SLA and fallback modes (e.g., on-site operation, local telemetry caching).

Risk 5: Diffusion of KPI computation logic across Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Telemetry sub-modules may introduce inconsistencies or data quality issues. Mitigation: Centralize KPI computation in NGHE Solution module and define telemetry validation rules.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Productivity_target value (m³/h or ton/h) and worksite safety plan constraints (geofencing, speed limits, policy rules) from Site Manager and Safety Manager.
Evidence 2: Vehicle cycle time SLA and sensor accuracy specifications from Vehicle supplier or design team.
Evidence 3: Teleoperation latency budget and command success rate requirements from Tele-operator and Human Factors team.
Evidence 4: Safety Services constraint enforcement rules and alert latency requirements from Safety Manager.
Evidence 5: Network Link bandwidth and latency specifications from IT/Network team.
Evidence 6: Representative operations trial plan (duration, worksite conditions, measurement procedures) from Project Manager and Site Manager.
Evidence 7: Telemetry data quality validation procedures and fallback measurement methods from System Engineer.
